why: Guards the filter-name dispatch in makeFilter (ASCIIHexDecode/AHx vs falling through to other filters), so a PDF stream declaring the ASCIIHexDecode filter (or its /AHx abbreviation) would flip this string-match branch that the fuzzer never hit.
Guards a documented workaround for broken PDF generators that emit generation=0xFFFFFFFF for the first xref-stream entry, a very specific and craftable cross-reference-stream field value.

Guards the special-cased IBM-patents xref bug where a classic xref table's first subsection erroneously starts at object 1 instead of 0, a concrete structural quirk of the plain-text xref table format.
Guards the trailer-dictionary /Prev key being encoded as an indirect reference ("/Prev NNN 0 R") instead of a plain integer, a specific alternate trailer syntax the parser special-cases.
Guards whether the cross-reference-stream dictionary's /Prev offset is large enough to require 64-bit storage (isInt64) rather than a normal int, directly exercising xref-chain offset-size handling.

Guards the stream-length-recovery path that resynchronizes by scanning forward for an actual "endstream" keyword when the declared /Length is wrong, a core PDF stream-framing repair decision that's easy to target with a mismatched Length value.
Guards validation that the catalog's /Pages entry is an indirect reference with an object number within the xref table's bounds, so a malformed or out-of-range /Pages ref would flip it.
Guards detection of a page tree whose actual number of leaf pages exceeds the declared top-level /Count, a genuine page-tree-count consistency check craftable via a mismatched /Count value.
